message:
test: add watcher debouncer tests and robot mode e2e tests

Adds comprehensive tests for the file watcher package and initial
end-to-end tests for robot mode output validation.

Watcher Tests (internal/watcher/):
- debouncer_test.go: Tests for NewDebouncer, Duration(), Trigger()
  - Single trigger coalescing
  - Multiple rapid triggers batched
  - Trigger after delay fires separately
  - Stop() cancels pending triggers
- watcher_test.go: Import fsnotify for test compatibility
  - Minor test cleanup (unused variable)

E2E Tests (tests/e2e/):
- robot_mode_test.go: Validates --robot-* flag JSON output
  - Tests --robot-status output structure
  - Tests --robot-version output structure
  - Validates JSON schema compliance
  - Skips gracefully when tmux unavailable

These tests ensure the watcher debouncing logic works correctly and
that robot mode output remains stable for automation consumers.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-zh1e","title":"Add scanner configuration system (.ntm.yaml)","description":"## Overview
Create a configuration system for UBS scanner integration, allowing customization of scan behavior, thresholds, and tool selection.

## Background
Different projects have different needs - some want strict scanning, others more lenient. A configuration system enables:
- Per-project customization
- Consistent behavior across team
- Easy toggling of features

## Implementation Details

### Config File Location
Search order:
1. .ntm.yaml in project root
2. ~/.config/ntm/config.yaml (global defaults)
3. Built-in defaults

### Config Schema
```yaml
scanner:
  # UBS executable path (auto-detected if not set)
  ubs_path: ~/projects/ultimate_bug_scanner/ubs
  
  # Default scan settings
  defaults:
    timeout: 60s
    parallel: true
    exclude:
      - vendor/**
      - node_modules/**
      - "*.test.go"
  
  # Severity thresholds for different contexts
  thresholds:
    pre_commit:
      block_critical: true
      block_errors: 5
    ci:
      fail_critical: true
      fail_errors: 0
    dashboard:
      show_warnings: true
      show_info: false
  
  # Tool selection (which UBS tools to run)
  tools:
    enabled:
      - semgrep
      - gosec
      - staticcheck
    disabled:
      - bandit  # Python not used in this project
  
  # Auto-bead creation settings
  beads:
    auto_create: true
    min_severity: error
    auto_close: true
    
  # Notification settings
  notifications:
    enabled: true
    on_new_critical: true
    summary_after_scan: true
```

### Files to Create/Modify
- internal/config/scanner.go - Scanner config types
- internal/config/loader.go - Config loading logic
- internal/scanner/config.go - Config integration

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config file parsed correctly
- [ ] Defaults applied when config missing
- [ ] Config validation with helpful errors
- [ ] Environment variable overrides supported","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlackSnow","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:34:11.668449-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T10:09:20.927634-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T10:09:20.927634-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-zh1e","depends_on_id":"ntm-p77j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:35:03.091094-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-zi5r","title":"EPIC: Session Analytics & Metrics","description":"# Session Analytics & Metrics

## Background & Motivation

Power users running many sessions need insights into their NTM usage patterns.

## CRITICAL: Honest About What We Can Track

**We can ONLY track NTM-initiated actions.** We cannot see:
- Agent-side API calls
- Actual token consumption
- What agents do between our prompts
- Agent "success" or "failure" (subjective)

**What we CAN track:**
- NTM commands executed
- Sessions created/destroyed
- Agents spawned/crashed
- Prompts sent via `ntm send`
- Session durations
- Pane activity (tmux data)

## What This Feature Provides

### 1. NTM Usage Statistics
```bash
ntm analytics
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ NTM Usage (Last 7 days)                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Sessions created:     42                                    │
│ Avg session duration: 2.3 hours                             │
│ Agents spawned:       168                                   │
│ Prompts sent (ntm send): 347                               │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ By Agent Type:                                              │
│   Claude:  89 spawned │ 203 prompts sent                   │
│   Codex:   52 spawned │ 98 prompts sent                    │
│   Gemini:  27 spawned │ 46 prompts sent                    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Peak usage: Weekdays 9-11am, 2-4pm                         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 2. Session History
```bash
ntm analytics sessions
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Recent Sessions                                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ api-refactor    │ 4h 12m │ 8 agents │ 42 prompts │ today  │
│ auth-feature    │ 2h 45m │ 6 agents │ 23 prompts │ today  │
│ test-coverage   │ 1h 30m │ 4 agents │ 15 prompts │ yday   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 3. Context Estimate Trends (Optional)
If Context Window Intelligence is enabled, show trends:
```bash
ntm analytics context
# Average context usage at session end: ~65%
# Sessions ended due to high context: 3
```

## What We're NOT Tracking

- ❌ Actual API token usage (we don't have access)
- ❌ Agent "success rate" (undefined metric)
- ❌ Code quality metrics (subjective)
- ❌ Prompt content (privacy, unless opted in)

## Storage

~/.config/ntm/analytics/events.jsonl
```json
{"ts":"2025-12-10T10:00:00Z","event":"session_create","session":"myproject"}
{"ts":"2025-12-10T10:01:00Z","event":"agent_spawn","session":"myproject","type":"cc","model":"opus"}
{"ts":"2025-12-10T10:05:00Z","event":"prompt_send","session":"myproject","target":"cc","chars":150}
```

30-day retention, automatic rotation.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Event logging for NTM actions
- [ ] ntm analytics shows usage summary
- [ ] Per-agent-type breakdowns
- [ ] Session history with duration
- [ ] Export to JSON/CSV
- [ ] Clear about what's tracked vs not
- [ ] 30-day retention policy","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:27:34.878202-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:36:04.340541-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-zxv","title":"Integrate Agent Mail into ntm status and dashboard","description":"# Integrate Agent Mail into ntm status and Dashboard

## Purpose

Show Agent Mail state (messages, locks) in the existing status and dashboard views for unified visibility.

## ntm status Enhancement

```
Session: myproject
├─ Panes: 5 (2 Claude, 2 Codex, 1 User)
├─ Created: 2 hours ago
├─ Attached: Yes
│
├─ Agent Mail: Connected ✓
│  ├─ Session Agent: ntm_myproject
│  ├─ Unread Messages: 3 (1 urgent)
│  └─ Active Locks: 2 patterns
│
└─ Pane Status:
   ├─ cc_1 (GreenCastle): 🔒 src/api/** │ 📬 2 unread
   ├─ cc_2 (BlueLake): idle
   ├─ cod_1 (RedStone): 🔒 tests/** │ 📭 no messages
   └─ ...
```

## Dashboard Integration

Add Agent Mail panel to dashboard view:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────┐
│ Panes (5)                           │ Agent Mail              │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤─────────────────────────┤
│ ● cc_1  GreenCastle  working        │ 📬 Messages             │
│ ● cc_2  BlueLake     idle           │  3 unread (1 urgent)    │
│ ● cod_1 RedStone     working        │                         │
│ ○ cod_2 -            -              │ 🔒 File Locks           │
│ ● user  -            -              │  2 active reservations  │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤                         │
│ [r]efresh [z]oom [m]ail [l]ocks     │ Press [m] for inbox     │
└─────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────┘
```

## Keyboard Shortcuts in Dashboard

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| m | Open mail inbox overlay |
| l | Show file locks overlay |
| M | Compose new message |
| L | Create new lock |

## Implementation Notes

- **Lazy loading**: Only fetch Agent Mail data if server available
- **Caching**: Cache inbox/locks for 30s to avoid excessive API calls
- **Badges**: Show unread count as badge on pane cards
- **Graceful degradation**: If Agent Mail unavailable, hide related UI

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ntm status shows Agent Mail connection status
- [ ] Per-pane agent name and message count displayed
- [ ] Dashboard has Agent Mail panel
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts for mail and locks
- [ ] Graceful handling when Agent Mail unavailable
- [ ] Cached data with refresh capability","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:43.880254-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:43.880254-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-zxv","depends_on_id":"ntm-161","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:56.191622-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-zxv","depends_on_id":"ntm-1ts","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:56.27531-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,151 @@
+package watcher
+
+import (
+	"sync/atomic"
+	"testing"
+	"time"
+)
+
+func TestNewDebouncer(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("default duration", func(t *testing.T) {
+		d := NewDebouncer(0)
+		if d.Duration() != DefaultDebounceDuration {
+			t.Errorf("Duration() = %v, want %v", d.Duration(), DefaultDebounceDuration)
+		}
+	})
+
+	t.Run("custom duration", func(t *testing.T) {
+		duration := 500 * time.Millisecond
+		d := NewDebouncer(duration)
+		if d.Duration() != duration {
+			t.Errorf("Duration() = %v, want %v", d.Duration(), duration)
+		}
+	})
+}
+
+func TestDebouncerTrigger(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("single trigger", func(t *testing.T) {
+		var callCount atomic.Int32
+		d := NewDebouncer(50 * time.Millisecond)
+
+		d.Trigger(func() {
+			callCount.Add(1)
+		})
+
+		// Wait for debounce
+		time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
+
+		if got := callCount.Load(); got != 1 {
+			t.Errorf("callback called %d times, want 1", got)
+		}
+	})
+
+	t.Run("multiple rapid triggers", func(t *testing.T) {
+		var callCount atomic.Int32
+		d := NewDebouncer(100 * time.Millisecond)
+
+		// Trigger multiple times rapidly
+		for i := 0; i < 5; i++ {
+			d.Trigger(func() {
+				callCount.Add(1)
+			})
+			time.Sleep(10 * time.Millisecond)
+		}
+
+		// Wait for debounce
+		time.Sleep(150 * time.Millisecond)
+
+		// Should only have been called once due to debouncing
+		if got := callCount.Load(); got != 1 {
+			t.Errorf("callback called %d times, want 1", got)
+		}
+	})
+
+	t.Run("triggers with delay between", func(t *testing.T) {
+		var callCount atomic.Int32
+		d := NewDebouncer(50 * time.Millisecond)
+
+		d.Trigger(func() {
+			callCount.Add(1)
+		})
+
+		// Wait for first to complete
+		time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
+
+		d.Trigger(func() {
+			callCount.Add(1)
+		})
+
+		// Wait for second to complete
+		time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
+
+		// Should have been called twice (once per non-overlapping trigger)
+		if got := callCount.Load(); got != 2 {
+			t.Errorf("callback called %d times, want 2", got)
+		}
+	})
+}
+
+func TestDebouncerCancel(t *testing.T) {
+	var callCount atomic.Int32
+	d := NewDebouncer(100 * time.Millisecond)
+
+	d.Trigger(func() {
+		callCount.Add(1)
+	})
+
+	// Cancel before debounce expires
+	time.Sleep(20 * time.Millisecond)
+	d.Cancel()
+
+	// Wait longer than debounce would have taken
+	time.Sleep(150 * time.Millisecond)
+
+	if got := callCount.Load(); got != 0 {
+		t.Errorf("callback called %d times after Cancel(), want 0", got)
+	}
+}
+
+func TestDebouncerCancelNilTimer(t *testing.T) {
+	d := NewDebouncer(50 * time.Millisecond)
+	// Cancel without any trigger should not panic
+	d.Cancel()
+}
+
+func TestDebouncerReset(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("changes duration", func(t *testing.T) {
+		d := NewDebouncer(100 * time.Millisecond)
+		newDuration := 200 * time.Millisecond
+		d.Reset(newDuration)
+
+		if d.Duration() != newDuration {
+			t.Errorf("Duration() = %v, want %v after Reset", d.Duration(), newDuration)
+		}
+	})
+
+	t.Run("cancels pending callback", func(t *testing.T) {
+		var callCount atomic.Int32
+		d := NewDebouncer(100 * time.Millisecond)
+
+		d.Trigger(func() {
+			callCount.Add(1)
+		})
+
+		// Reset cancels the pending trigger
+		time.Sleep(20 * time.Millisecond)
+		d.Reset(50 * time.Millisecond)
+
+		// Wait longer than both durations
+		time.Sleep(200 * time.Millisecond)
+
+		if got := callCount.Load(); got != 0 {
+			t.Errorf("callback called %d times after Reset(), want 0", got)
+		}
+	})
+}
+
+func TestDefaultDebounceDuration(t *testing.T) {
+	if DefaultDebounceDuration != 250*time.Millisecond {
+		t.Errorf("DefaultDebounceDuration = %v, want 250ms", DefaultDebounceDuration)
+	}
+}
